metadata.description: Progressive certification in the Informativ Method — the insight-led selling framework that drives Informativ's go-to-market execution. 12 modules covering rep profiles, commercial insights, stakeholder tailoring, dealer archetypes, constructive tension, process discipline, the six-step teaching pitch, objection reframing, self-assessment, pipeline diagnostics, and the 90-day operating system. Each module includes Prep with Chuck for guided learning before the quiz.

modules.title: What Makes Insight Leaders Different
modules.description: Validates understanding of the five rep profiles, why Insight Leaders outperform in complex deals, and how profile awareness shapes sales strategy.
modules.prep_focus: Understand the five rep profiles (Insight Leader, Relationship Builder, Hard Worker, Lone Wolf, Problem Solver), why Insight Leaders win 39% of complex deals vs. 22% for Relationship Builders, and what separates teaching from transactional selling in the automotive dealership context.

questions.question: According to research cited in the Informativ Method, what percentage of complex deals do Insight Leaders win compared to Relationship Builders?

options.A: Insight Leaders win 25% vs. Relationship Builders at 18%
options.B: Insight Leaders win 39% vs. Relationship Builders at 22%
options.C: Insight Leaders win 45% vs. Relationship Builders at 30%
options.D: Both profiles win at roughly equal rates in complex deals

questions.question: Which rep profile relies on instinct over process and delivers inconsistent results?

options.A: Relationship Builder
options.B: Hard Worker
options.C: Lone Wolf
options.D: Problem Solver

questions.question: What three things does the Insight Leader approach create for enterprise buyers?

options.A: Permission to lead, differentiation versus cheaper alternatives, and urgency around solvable problems
options.B: Deep personal relationships, product discounts, and long implementation timelines
options.C: Competitive pricing, feature comparisons, and contract flexibility
options.D: Quick demos, trial periods, and referral incentives

questions.question: A rep who executes high activity volume but lacks teaching insights is best described as which profile?

options.A: Insight Leader
options.B: Lone Wolf
options.C: Hard Worker
options.D: Problem Solver

questions.question: Why does the Informativ Method say that relationships alone are insufficient for complex deals like the Total Solution?

options.A: Dealers don't value personal relationships
options.B: Complex deals involve multiple stakeholders with conflicting priorities and competing vendors
options.C: Relationship selling is outdated in all industries
options.D: Informativ's products are too technical for relationship-based selling

questions.question: What is the primary diagnostic exercise for Week 1 of the Informativ Method?

options.A: Build three commercial insights for your pipeline
options.B: Map all stakeholders in your top deal
options.C: Identify your dominant profile under pressure and examine your three largest pipeline deals for Insight Leader patterns
options.D: Draft a teaching pitch for your biggest opportunity

modules.title: Lead with Commercial Insights
modules.description: Tests ability to construct and deliver commercial insights using the Data → Pain Connection → Solvable Problem structure.
modules.prep_focus: Master the Data → Pain Connection → Solvable Problem structure. Learn to build dealer-specific insights (credit pull friction, compliance gaps, misclassification costs) that prove homework, create tension around unknown problems, and position you as a teacher rather than a vendor.

questions.question: What is the correct structure of a commercial insight in the Informativ Method?

options.A: Feature → Benefit → Close
options.B: Data → Pain Connection → Solvable Problem
options.C: Problem → Solution → Pricing
options.D: Need → Demo → Proposal

questions.question: Which of the following is a properly structured commercial insight?

options.A: Informativ has the best compliance tools in the market
options.B: Our platform integrates with all major DMS systems
options.C: Dealers without integrated compliance document management see 18% lower F&I attachment rates because they miss cross-sell opportunities and create audit risk
options.D: We can save you money on credit bureau costs

questions.question: What three things does a commercial insight accomplish?

options.A: Builds rapport, creates urgency, closes the deal
options.B: Proves you've done homework, creates tension around an unknown problem, positions you as a teacher
options.C: Demonstrates product features, provides pricing, asks for the sale
options.D: Establishes likability, shows industry experience, offers a discount

questions.question: According to the Informativ Method, what is the hidden annual cost to dealerships from misclassifying loans?

options.A: $5K-10K in audit fines
options.B: $15K-25K in hidden productivity loss
options.C: $50K-75K in compliance penalties
options.D: $100K+ in lost revenue

questions.question: Without commercial insights, what does the Informativ Method say you become?

options.A: A trusted advisor
options.B: A strategic partner
options.C: Either an order-taker or an aggressive closer
options.D: A product expert

questions.question: What is the difference between an industry statistic and a commercial insight?

options.A: An industry statistic is more accurate
options.B: A commercial insight changes how the dealer thinks about their own business and creates permission to lead
options.C: There is no real difference — both are equally effective
options.D: Industry statistics work better for large dealer groups

modules.title: Know Your Buyers — Stakeholder Personas
modules.description: Tests knowledge of the five key dealership stakeholder personas and the ability to tailor messaging to each buyer's priorities.
modules.prep_focus: Map the five dealership stakeholders (Principal, GM, F&I Director, IT/CTO, Controller/CFO), understand what each cares about, learn the diagnostic question 'Who else needs to be comfortable with this change?', and recognize why deals stall when you only sell to one person.

questions.question: What does the Dealer Principal/Owner care about most when evaluating a vendor like Informativ?

options.A: Integration complexity and data security
options.B: Competitive positioning, enterprise margin impact, and risk exposure
options.C: F&I attachment rates and lender relationships
options.D: Cost reduction and ROI transparency

questions.question: The F&I Director thinks in terms of which primary concern?

options.A: Does this strengthen or weaken our market position?
options.B: Will this slow down my team or speed us up?
options.C: Does this reduce audit risk and increase deal profitability?
options.D: How much implementation lift and what's our uptime risk?

questions.question: What is the key diagnostic question for identifying all stakeholders in a complex deal?

options.A: Who signs the contract?
options.B: Who controls the budget?
options.C: Who else needs to be comfortable with this change?
options.D: Who is the most senior person at the dealership?

questions.question: According to the Informativ Method, why do most reps' deals stall in committee?

options.A: The product doesn't have enough features
options.B: Pricing is too high for the dealership's budget
options.C: The rep undersold to all but one stakeholder and relied on that person to sell internally
options.D: The dealer group requires corporate approval for all technology purchases

questions.question: The IT/CTO stakeholder evaluates Informativ based on which criteria?

options.A: Compliance liability and product penetration
options.B: Integration complexity, data security, and vendor consolidation
options.C: Deal velocity and staff adoption
options.D: Cost reduction and audit readiness

questions.question: What does the General Manager think in terms of when evaluating a new platform?

options.A: Does this strengthen or weaken our market position?
options.B: Does this reduce audit risk and increase deal profitability?
options.C: Will this slow down my team or speed us up?
options.D: Where's the payback and can we justify it to the board?

modules.title: Dealer Archetypes and Tailoring
modules.description: Tests understanding of the five dealer archetypes and how to adapt pitch, deal structure, and success metrics to each business model.
modules.prep_focus: Learn the five dealer archetypes (franchised, independent, dealer group, large franchise group, eCommerce-first), which Informativ products to lead with for each, and how to adjust deal size, timeline, and success metrics by business model.

questions.question: Which dealer archetype prioritizes quick ROI proof, operates on slim margins, and needs payback in months?

options.A: Franchised dealership
options.B: Independent used car dealer
options.C: Dealer group / multi-location
options.D: eCommerce-first dealer

questions.question: What should you emphasize when selling to a franchised dealership?

options.A: SmartPencil ROI and 90-day implementation
options.B: API integrations and mobile optimization
options.C: Total Solution's compliance framework and Informativ Compliance module
options.D: Cross-location reporting and consolidated credit pull management

questions.question: Dealer groups and multi-location operations buy based on which primary driver?

options.A: Quick wins and simple solutions
options.B: Standardization, governance frameworks, and cross-location efficiency
options.C: API flexibility and mobile-first design
options.D: Premium support and regulatory compliance

questions.question: For an eCommerce-first dealer, which Informativ product and capability should you highlight?

options.A: Informativ Compliance module and audit-ready reporting
options.B: Verified STIPs' vendor consolidation
options.C: API integrations and mobile optimization of eZ Form Fill
options.D: Informativ Customer Insights cross-location reporting

questions.question: What is the Insight Leader's common mistake when it comes to dealer archetypes?

options.A: Using too many data points in the pitch
options.B: Not adjusting deal size and timeline expectations by dealer type
options.C: Spending too much time on discovery
options.D: Over-tailoring the message to each stakeholder

questions.question: When selling to an independent used car dealer, what approach does the Informativ Method recommend?

options.A: Lead with Total Solution's enterprise compliance framework
options.B: Discuss governance frameworks and consolidated architecture
options.C: Lead with SmartPencil ROI and 90-day implementation
options.D: Emphasize API flexibility and digital tool stacking

modules.title: Constructive Tension — Taking Control Part 1
modules.description: Tests understanding of constructive tension levers and the ability to create clarity and urgency without aggression.
modules.prep_focus: Master the five constructive tension levers: setting agendas upfront, naming next steps explicitly, pushing back on vague timelines, reframing price conversations with math, and asking for the business directly. Understand why ambiguity kills deals and tension is clarity, not aggression.

questions.question: According to the Informativ Method, what is constructive tension?

options.A: Applying pressure to force a decision
options.B: Clarity plus urgency — naming what's real so you can move forward
options.C: Creating artificial scarcity around your product
options.D: Telling the dealer their current vendor is inferior

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of naming next steps explicitly?

options.A: We'll stay in touch and circle back when you're ready
options.B: Let me know if you have any questions
options.C: By Tuesday, you'll connect with your IT team and confirm integration complexity. I'll prep technical requirements and circle back Wednesday at 2pm.
options.D: I'll follow up sometime next week to see where things stand

questions.question: How does the Informativ Method recommend reframing a price conversation?

options.A: Offer a discount to match the competitor
options.B: Compare what the dealer is losing today versus what Informativ costs — show the math
options.C: Explain that quality costs more
options.D: Defer the pricing discussion to a later meeting

questions.question: What does the Informativ Method say happens to deals without tension?

options.A: They close eventually on their own
options.B: They drift forever — you either create urgency or lose
options.C: The dealer respects your patience and rewards it
options.D: They stall briefly but recover with a strong demo

questions.question: When a dealer says 'We'll revisit in Q2,' what does the Informativ Method say you should do?

options.A: Accept the timeline and schedule a follow-up in Q2
options.B: Ask 'What would need to happen for you to decide by [date]?' to force them to name the real constraint
options.C: Send a follow-up email with additional case studies
options.D: Escalate to their principal for a faster decision

questions.question: What is the most common mistake reps make around tension?

options.A: Creating too much urgency
options.B: Being too aggressive with decision timelines
options.C: Avoiding tension because they fear rejection
options.D: Asking for the business too early in the process

modules.title: Process Discipline — Taking Control Part 2
modules.description: Tests knowledge of the three-phase sales process, red flags for losing control, and how process discipline compounds into pipeline velocity.
modules.prep_focus: Learn the three-phase sales process (Discovery → Demo → Proposal), the four Discovery elements, proposal cadence (48hrs → 3-day review → decision date), red flags for lost control, and how structured reps close 20% more deals.

questions.question: What are the three phases of Informativ's sales process?

options.A: Prospect → Pitch → Close
options.B: Discovery → Demo → Proposal
options.C: Qualify → Present → Negotiate
options.D: Research → Engage → Contract

questions.question: During Discovery, which four elements should a rep establish?

options.A: Budget, authority, need, timeline
options.B: Features, benefits, pricing, contract terms
options.C: Objectives, stakeholder mapping, timeline, decision criteria
options.D: Pain points, competitive landscape, product fit, implementation plan

questions.question: Which of the following is a red flag that you've lost control of a deal?

options.A: The dealer asks for a second demo for additional stakeholders
options.B: The dealer says 'We'll circle back next month' with no defined timeline
options.C: The dealer requests a formal proposal
options.D: The dealer introduces you to their IT team

questions.question: What is the correct proposal cadence in the Informativ Method?

options.A: Send proposal immediately after the demo and wait for response
options.B: 48 hours to present → 3-day review period → explicit decision date
options.C: One week to customize proposal → two-week review → follow up monthly
options.D: Send proposal before the demo so the dealer can review pricing

questions.question: How does the Informativ Method say demos should be conducted?

options.A: Walk through every feature of the platform comprehensively
options.B: A targeted proof that your solution solves their specific problem — not a feature dump
options.C: Let the dealer drive the demo and explore what interests them
options.D: Focus primarily on pricing and competitive comparisons

questions.question: According to the Informativ Method, reps who propose on a firm timeline with a decision date close how many more deals?

options.A: 10% more deals
options.B: 15% more deals
options.C: 20% more deals
options.D: 30% more deals

modules.title: Teaching Pitch Part 1 — Warmer, Reframe, Rational Drowning
modules.description: Tests mastery of the first three steps of the six-step teaching pitch: opening with credibility, delivering the reframe, and stacking data.
modules.prep_focus: Master the first three steps of the teaching pitch: Warmer (30 sec — dealer agreement and credibility), Reframe (30 sec — the aha moment), and Rational Drowning (2 min — stacking specific data points). Learn the permission check: 'Does that resonate?'

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Warmer in the teaching pitch?

options.A: To introduce yourself and your company
options.B: To open with dealer agreement and build credibility by meeting them where they are
options.C: To present your product's key features
options.D: To establish pricing expectations early

questions.question: What does the Reframe accomplish in the teaching pitch?

options.A: It presents product pricing in a new way
options.B: It delivers the aha moment — making the dealer see the problem differently
options.C: It summarizes the competitive landscape
options.D: It introduces Informativ's product line

questions.question: How long should the Rational Drowning section last?

options.A: 30 seconds
options.B: 1 minute
options.C: 2 minutes
options.D: 5 minutes

questions.question: What is the critical step after completing Rational Drowning?

options.A: Immediately present your product
options.B: Ask for the business
options.C: Check for permission — 'Does that resonate?' — and pivot if they say no
options.D: Move to a product demo

questions.question: Which of the following is a correctly structured Warmer for an Informativ pitch?

options.A: Informativ is the leading compliance platform for automotive dealerships
options.B: Every dealer we talk to is wrestling with rising credit bureau costs and tighter F&I margins, and most are solving it the way you probably are
options.C: I'd like to show you how Informativ can save you money
options.D: We have the most comprehensive compliance solution in the market

questions.question: What is the purpose of stacking multiple data points during Rational Drowning?

options.A: To overwhelm the dealer with information so they feel they need help
options.B: To build unquestionable truth through specific, sourced data tied to their pain
options.C: To demonstrate your industry knowledge and research capability
options.D: To fill time before the product demo

modules.title: Teaching Pitch Part 2 — Emotional Impact, New Way, Your Solution
modules.description: Tests mastery of the final three steps of the six-step teaching pitch: personalizing consequences, painting vision, and positioning Informativ.
modules.prep_focus: Complete the six-step pitch: Emotional Impact (personalize the cost — 'That's someone's college fund'), A New Way (paint vision WITHOUT naming the product), Your Solution (here's your future → here's how we make it real → here's proof). Full pitch takes 8-10 minutes and ends with permission check.

questions.question: Why is Emotional Impact necessary after you've already presented rational data?

options.A: Numbers alone don't create behavior change — emotion personalizes the consequence
options.B: It gives you time to prepare the next section of the pitch
options.C: Dealers don't understand data without emotional context
options.D: It's a required step in all sales methodologies

questions.question: What is the correct approach for the 'A New Way' section?

options.A: Name your product and explain its key features
options.B: Compare your solution to the dealer's current vendor
options.C: Paint a vision of the dealer's future WITHOUT naming your product
options.D: Present a case study from a similar dealership

questions.question: How long should the complete six-step teaching pitch take?

options.A: 3-5 minutes
options.B: 8-10 minutes
options.C: 15-20 minutes
options.D: 30 minutes

questions.question: What is the correct sequence of the six-step teaching pitch?

options.A: Warmer → Reframe → Emotional Impact → Rational Drowning → A New Way → Your Solution
options.B: Warmer → Rational Drowning → Reframe → A New Way → Emotional Impact → Your Solution
options.C: Warmer → Reframe → Rational Drowning → Emotional Impact → A New Way → Your Solution
options.D: Reframe → Warmer → Rational Drowning → Your Solution → Emotional Impact → A New Way

questions.question: How should the teaching pitch end?

options.A: With a pricing proposal
options.B: By scheduling a follow-up demo
options.C: With a permission check: 'Does this approach make sense to you?'
options.D: By asking the dealer to sign a letter of intent

questions.question: The 'Your Solution' step should include which three elements?

options.A: Pricing, contract terms, and implementation timeline
options.B: Here's your future → here's how we make it real → here's proof it works
options.C: Product overview → feature list → competitive comparison
options.D: Demo invitation → trial offer → volume discount

modules.title: Objections as Teaching Opportunities
modules.description: Tests the objection reframing framework and the ability to turn resistance into teaching moments that advance the deal.
modules.prep_focus: Master the Hear → Validate → Reframe → Teach → Next Step framework. Learn to reframe the four common objections: price (show the math), timing (force the real constraint), no-need (teach the gap they don't know), and scope (separate must-have from nice-to-have, start with Protect).

questions.question: What is the Informativ Method's objection handling framework?

options.A: Acknowledge → Overcome → Close
options.B: Hear → Validate → Reframe → Teach → Next Step
options.C: Listen → Respond → Discount → Ask Again
options.D: Empathize → Explain → Redirect

questions.question: When a dealer says 'You're too expensive,' what should you do?

options.A: Justify the price by listing features
options.B: Offer a discount to match their budget
options.C: Never justify — compare what they're losing today versus what Informativ costs, and show the math
options.D: Explain that your product is premium and worth the investment

questions.question: A dealer says 'Our current process works fine.' According to the Informativ Method, what is the best response approach?

options.A: Respect their position and move on to the next prospect
options.B: Validate their belief, then teach them about a gap they didn't know they had — like compliance exposure or F&I attachment impact
options.C: Tell them their process is actually broken based on industry data
options.D: Ask to speak with someone else at the dealership who might see it differently

questions.question: When a dealer says they can't afford the full solution, how should you reframe the scope objection?

options.A: Apply a volume discount to make the full solution affordable
options.B: Tell them the full solution is the only option that makes sense
options.C: Separate must-have now from nice-to-have later — start with Protect and upgrade to Enrich in six months
options.D: Suggest they wait until their budget allows the full purchase

questions.question: What three things does every objection reframe accomplish?

options.A: Reduces price sensitivity, extends timeline, maintains rapport
options.B: Proves you're not defensive, creates a teaching moment, moves the deal forward or reveals the real issue
options.C: Demonstrates product knowledge, builds credibility, closes the deal
options.D: Validates the concern, offers alternatives, asks for commitment

questions.question: For a timing objection ('We're not ready yet'), the Informativ Method says you should never accept that. Instead, you should:

options.A: Respect their timeline and follow up quarterly
options.B: Offer an early-sign discount to create urgency
options.C: Ask 'What would need to happen for you to move forward now?' and 'What do you give up by waiting six months?'
options.D: Send case studies showing how other dealers benefited from acting quickly

modules.title: Your Rep Profile and Gaps — Self-Assessment
modules.description: Tests understanding of profile awareness under pressure, behavioral defaults, and how to build guardrails against regression.
modules.prep_focus: Understand your default profile and your pressure profile (what you revert to when behind on quota). Learn guardrails: Relationship Builders need process discipline partners, Lone Wolves need monthly pipeline reviews, Hard Workers need teaching point libraries. Development approach: one behavior, two weeks, measure, repeat.

questions.question: According to the Informativ Method, what happens to most reps under pressure or when behind on quota?

options.A: They perform at a higher level due to urgency
options.B: They revert to a default profile that may not serve them in complex deals
options.C: They become more disciplined and process-oriented
options.D: They ask for more coaching and support

questions.question: A rep who naturally leads with Insight Leader behaviors but reverts to avoiding tension when a big deal gets stressful is defaulting to which profile?

options.A: Hard Worker
options.B: Lone Wolf
options.C: Relationship Builder
options.D: Problem Solver

questions.question: If a rep's natural style is Lone Wolf, what guardrail does the Informativ Method recommend?

options.A: Build a library of teaching points
options.B: Find a process discipline partner to push back
options.C: Monthly pipeline reviews to ground instinct in structure
options.D: Practice the teaching pitch until it becomes second nature

questions.question: What is the Informativ Method's recommended development approach?

options.A: Work on all five profiles simultaneously
options.B: Pick one behavior that doesn't come naturally, practice it deliberately for two weeks, measure it, and repeat
options.C: Focus only on closing techniques for the next quarter
options.D: Attend additional product training to strengthen weak areas

questions.question: A Hard Worker profile without insight needs which specific guardrail?

options.A: Monthly pipeline reviews
options.B: A process discipline partner
options.C: A library of teaching points to convert activity into insight-led conversations
options.D: Reduced call volume to focus on quality

questions.question: Why does the Informativ Method emphasize knowing your pressure profile?

options.A: So you can avoid stressful deals
options.B: So your manager can assign deals based on your strengths
options.C: Because everyone has a default they fall back to under stress, and awareness lets you build guardrails
options.D: So you can explain to dealers why you sell the way you do

modules.title: Pipeline Through the Informativ Method Lens
modules.description: Tests ability to diagnose stuck deals using the Teach-Tailor-Control framework and identify the specific intervention needed.
modules.prep_focus: Learn to diagnose every stuck deal with three questions: What did I teach? Who have I tailored to? What happens next and when? No Teaching = no differentiation (price shopping). No Tailoring = stalled in committee. No Control = indefinite drift. Insight Leaders treat stuck deals as diagnostic opportunities, not lost causes.

questions.question: According to the Informativ Method, every stuck deal fails on which dimension?

options.A: Price, timing, or competition
options.B: Teach, Tailor, or Control
options.C: Discovery, demo, or proposal
options.D: Stakeholder, budget, or need

questions.question: A deal where the dealer thinks you're one of three vendors saying the same thing and is shopping on price is missing which element?

options.A: Tailoring — you haven't connected with enough stakeholders
options.B: Teaching — the deal lacks differentiation because you haven't delivered a unique insight
options.C: Control — you don't have a clear timeline
options.D: All three elements equally

questions.question: What are the three diagnostic questions for any stuck deal?

options.A: What's our price? What's their budget? Who decides?
options.B: What did I teach? Who have I tailored to? What happens next and when?
options.C: What features do they need? What's the competition? When is the contract up?
options.D: Who is the champion? What's their pain? How do we close?

questions.question: A deal that stalled in committee because the principal doesn't understand competitive advantage and IT thinks integration is risky is missing which element?

options.A: Teaching
options.B: Tailoring — key stakeholders don't see relevance to their specific priorities
options.C: Control
options.D: Pricing — the deal needs a discount to move forward

questions.question: If you can't name a specific next step with a date on a deal, what does the Informativ Method say?

options.A: The deal needs more discovery
options.B: You need to re-demo the product
options.C: You don't have control — set a specific next step immediately
options.D: The deal is likely lost and should be removed from pipeline

questions.question: How do Insight Leaders treat stuck deals differently from most reps?

options.A: They discount to unstick them
options.B: They escalate to management for help
options.C: They treat them as diagnostic opportunities — identify the missing element and fix it
options.D: They move on to new prospects and revisit later

modules.title: 90-Day Informativ Method Action Plan — Capstone
modules.description: Tests knowledge of the five toolkit components, the weekly-monthly-quarterly operating rhythm, and the three core metrics that drive pipeline velocity.
modules.prep_focus: Build your operating system: five toolkit components (Owned Insights, Stakeholder Templates, Teaching Pitches, Objection Library, Red-Flag List), weekly rhythm (Friday deal review), monthly rhythm (one-on-one coaching on one Teach-Tailor-Control skill), quarterly refresh. Track three metrics: % deals with teaching moment, % with multi-stakeholder tailoring, % with named next step and date.

questions.question: What are the five components of the Informativ Method Toolkit?

options.A: CRM setup, call scripts, email templates, proposal templates, pricing sheets
options.B: Owned Insights, Stakeholder Tailor Templates, Teaching Pitches Per Product, Objection Response Library, Red-Flag List
options.C: Pipeline reports, forecast models, competitive analysis, customer stories, ROI calculators
options.D: Product demos, case studies, references, trial plans, implementation guides

questions.question: What is the recommended weekly rhythm in the Informativ Method operating system?

options.A: Monday pipeline review with manager
options.B: Daily call activity reporting
options.C: Friday deal review: close-won analysis using Teach-Tailor-Control lens, plus stuck-deal diagnostics
options.D: Weekly team meeting to discuss competitive intelligence

questions.question: What are the three metrics the Informativ Method says you should track to drive pipeline velocity?

options.A: Calls made, demos scheduled, proposals sent
options.B: Win rate, average deal size, sales cycle length
options.C: Percentage of deals with documented teaching moments, documented tailor to multiple stakeholders, and named next steps with dates
options.D: Revenue booked, pipeline created, quota attainment

questions.question: How many Owned Insights should a rep maintain in their toolkit?

options.A: 1 per product in the portfolio
options.B: 2-3, specific to their pipeline and personas, reviewed monthly
options.C: 5-10 covering all possible dealer scenarios
options.D: As many as possible to address any situation

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Red-Flag List in the toolkit?

options.A: To track competitors' weaknesses
options.B: To identify deals that should be disqualified from the pipeline
options.C: To recognize warning signs you've lost control — indefinite timeline, no sponsor, fuzzy committee, three-month pause
options.D: To flag compliance issues in dealer accounts

questions.question: The Informativ Method says this program is not a course you complete — it's:

options.A: A certification you renew annually
options.B: A philosophy you embrace
options.C: A system you use forever, with weekly-monthly-quarterly rhythms driving permanent behavior change
options.D: A framework that evolves with each product release
